rationale: No regression under the DAG/merge-signal lens, verified by region diffs of 3ca2cd2c vs bf01a21a. The frontmatter is byte-identical (1→2→3 edges and every key_file_paths set unchanged) and Bead 1 is byte-identical. Bead 3's only delta is word-boundary anchoring on its two /ms-panel greps — a falsifiability strengthening, no coupling change; its runbook.md doc-sync gate survives, as do all three disjoint doc-sync targets (p2 lines 355/616/743). §606's upstream content — the 110 and 112 bullets and the mechanical-rebase paragraph — is untouched; only the platform-schema paragraph was rewritten, which is orthogonal to upstream plans and consumes no 110/112 symbol. Every repo path in the added lines already sits inside Bead 2's or Bead 3's existing key sets; the buildCommand chokepoint, hardening, parsing, and codex shim all live in ms-panel.js/workflow_test.go or /tmp e2e scaffolding, adding no cross-bead or cross-spec coupling.

findings.title: Delta containment verified structurally, not just by prose claim
findings.detail: Region diffs confirm the BRIEF's containment claim: frontmatter (work_chunks, depends_on, key_file_paths) and Bead 1 byte-identical; Bead 3 changed only in its two verification grep lines (now `grep -Eq '/ms-panel([^-a-z]|$)'`); the Risks/Sequencing section changed only in the platform-claim paragraph ('Return-value schema is not an on-disk-artifact guarantee'), leaving the 110/112 surface claims, rebase points, and mechanical-rebase-safety analysis I verified in round 1 word-for-word intact. Provenance rows updated consistently with the strengthened Bead 2 proofs. New e2e machinery (branch-built binary on PATH at /tmp/ms111, codex PATH-shim at /tmp/ms111-shim with MS111_CODEX_SCENARIO) is non-repo scratch, so no new tracked files and no key_file_paths drift.

findings.title: Round-1 wording nit stands, still non-blocking
findings.detail: My round-1 info finding on §606's 'only overlap' enumeration (it omits the SKILL.md and domain-doc overlaps with 110/112 that the plan itself flags elsewhere) is unchanged by this fix — expected, since no round-1 RC targeted it. It remains cosmetic: the forward-rebase precedes bead authoring, so the operative clean-rebase claim holds.
